description: Golden dataset with 3 independently verifiable programs from well-known institutions. Each value is traceable from Silver source row through Gold derivation to expected output. Selected programs: MIT Computer Science (high earnings CS), UPenn Nursing (moderate earnings healthcare), UF Business Administration (typical business program for debt-to-earnings math verification).

values.description: MIT Computer Science — 1yr median earnings (highest-earning CS program, verifiable against College Scorecard public data)

traceability.silver_source: base.college_scorecard WHERE unitid=166683 AND cipcode='11.07' AND credential_level=3
traceability.derivation: Direct copy from Silver earnings_1yr_median — no transformation applied
traceability.verification_note: MIT CS is one of the highest-earning bachelor's programs in the country. $118K median 1yr earnings is consistent with publicly reported College Scorecard data for MIT CIPCODE 11.07.

values.description: MIT Computer Science — debt-to-earnings ratio (verifies division: 11077 / 118191 = 0.0937)

traceability.silver_source: base.college_scorecard WHERE unitid=166683 AND cipcode='11.07' — debt_median=11077.0, earnings_1yr_median=118191.0
traceability.verification_note: MIT's low debt ($11K) relative to high CS earnings ($118K) produces a very low DTE ratio of ~0.094, well within the 'Low' tier. This is independently verifiable: MIT has need-blind admission with generous financial aid, explaining the low median debt.

values.description: MIT Computer Science — debt-to-earnings tier (verifies tier bucketing logic for 'Low' tier)

traceability.derivation: debt_to_earnings_annual = 0.0937 < 0.75 => 'Low' per CASE WHEN logic in spec
traceability.verification_note: Ratio of 0.094 is far below the 0.75 threshold for 'Low' tier.

values.description: MIT Computer Science — confidence tier (verifies derivation: large cohort + has earnings + has debt = 'high')

traceability.silver_source: small_cohort_flag=False, earnings_1yr_median=118191 (not null), earnings_2yr_median=127993 (not null), debt_median=11077 (not null)
traceability.verification_note: MIT CS has 424 completions (well above 30 threshold) and all three outcome fields populated.

values.description: MIT Computer Science — program value index (verifies inverse: 118191 / 11077 = 10.67)

traceability.derivation: earnings_1yr_median / debt_median = 118191.0 / 11077.0 = 10.6699. This is the mathematical inverse of debt_to_earnings_annual (1/0.0937 = 10.67).
traceability.verification_note: PVI of 10.67 means MIT CS grads earn ~10.7x their debt in the first year — exceptional ROI.

values.description: UPenn Nursing — 1yr median earnings (moderate earnings healthcare program)

traceability.silver_source: base.college_scorecard WHERE unitid=215062 AND cipcode='51.38' AND credential_level=3
traceability.verification_note: UPenn BSN graduates earning ~$68K in year 1 is consistent with Philadelphia-area RN salaries and College Scorecard public data.
UPenn Nursing has higher debt ($23K) than MIT but strong nursing earnings, yielding DTE of 0.34 — still comfortably in the 'Low' tier.

values.description: UPenn Nursing — earnings growth rate (verifies cross-cohort: (70009 - 68279) / 68279 = 0.0253)

traceability.verification_note: Modest 2.5% positive differential between 1yr and 2yr cohorts. This is a cross-cohort comparison, not longitudinal growth.

values.description: UPenn Nursing — outcome completeness (all 3 fields present = 1.0)

traceability.derivation: earnings_1yr_median (68279, not null) + earnings_2yr_median (70009, not null) + debt_median (23288, not null) = 3 non-null / 3 = 1.0
traceability.verification_note: All three core outcome fields are populated, so completeness is exactly 1.0.

traceability.silver_source: base.college_scorecard WHERE unitid=134130 AND cipcode='52.02' — debt_median=15000.0, earnings_1yr_median=46557.0
traceability.verification_note: UF Business at a large public university shows $15K debt vs $46.5K earnings — typical 'Low' DTE for a state school business program.

values.description: UF Business Admin — program value index (inverse of DTE: 46557 / 15000 = 3.1038)

traceability.verification_note: PVI of 3.1 means first-year earnings are about 3x the total debt — a solid value proposition for a state university business degree. Inverse of DTE: 1/0.3222 = 3.1038.

values.description: UF Business Admin — earnings growth rate (negative value: (44821 - 46557) / 46557 = -0.0373)

traceability.verification_note: Negative growth rate (-3.7%) is expected and valid — the 1yr and 2yr figures come from different graduating cohorts, not the same people tracked over time. About 44% of programs show this pattern.
